avalancheActivity.comment:
Up to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last three days above approximately 1600 m. Little snow fell during the night.
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate northeasterly wind, sometimes deep wind slabs will form by the early morning in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls as well as at high altitude. In particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation and on very steep slopes small and medium-sized avalanches are possible. They can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible below approximately 2400 m. This applies in particular in case of releases originating from steep sunny starting zones at intermediate altitudes that have retained the snow thus far. Even a small snow slide can sweep people along and give rise to falls.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow, and up to 30 cm in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
The new snow is lying on a crust and generally at low and intermediate altitudes.
High Alpine regions: The moderate wind has transported the new snow.
Over a wide area there is still a very large amount of snow.

avalancheActivity.comment:
In some regions up to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last three days above approximately 1800 m. The new snow is lying on top of a quite favourable old snowpack.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads. This applies especially on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day individual small moist and wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on extremely steep sunny slopes. Even a small snow slide can sweep people along and give rise to falls.
avalancheActivity.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions are mostly favourable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 15 cm of snow fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from the middle of the day to gradual moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes at intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
